summary: The test suite has critical gaps that would give false confidence. Several tests don't actually call the functions they claim to test: 'Invoke-CleanupPlan' constructs a manual hashtable instead of calling the function; 'Invoke-Cleanup' tests just assert that a local boolean variable equals itself (true/false) without ever invoking Invoke-Cleanup; 'Test-ArtifactRetention-MaxSize' manually sorts an array and checks element order without calling Invoke-CleanupPlan at all. Of the five core requirements, only max-age policy, keep-latest-N policy, and Format-CleanupSummary are genuinely exercised through the real functions. Dry-run mode is never actually tested through Invoke-Cleanup. No edge cases are covered: empty artifact lists, artifacts exactly at age/size boundaries, combined policy interactions, workflows with zero artifacts, or error/graceful-failure scenarios. The BeforeAll dot-source setup is correct and tests are readable, but the misleading test bodies (trivially true assertions masquerading as functional tests) make the suite unreliable for regression detection.
